methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $12M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 25.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
HEO is a Series A-stage Space & Aerospace company founded by William Crowe and Hiranya Jayakody that has raised $12M to date. The valuation universe comprises 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Sierra Space, and Planet Labs, though a sufficiently tight peer cohort by stage and valuation tier was not available for direct median benchmarking.

Given data constraints, stage-progression methodology is the primary applicable approach. Using total capital raised ($12M) as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement yields an implied valuation of $24M. Public comps in Space & Aerospace trade at a median 25.8x P/S across the five tickers, but this method cannot be deployed absent the target's revenue disclosure. No private peer set of sufficient size and homogeneity met filtering criteria for peer-median triangulation.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $20M to $28M, centered on $24M. This range reflects limited methodological confirmation—only the stage-progression method applied cleanly. Key uncertainties include: HEO's revenue run rate, which would enable public-comp anchoring; the availability of more granular pricing data from private comparables at identical stage; and the relevance of the 2.0x multiplier to HEO's specific growth profile and capital efficiency relative to sector norms. Investors should require revenue visibility and detailed comparable transaction metrics before narrowing this range.
